User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the QFX Finance platform, extending authentication with KYC profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's profile avatar.

kycStatus.description: The Know Your Customer (KYC) verification status of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

FiatAccount.title: FiatAccount
FiatAccount.description: Represents a traditional banking (fiat) account held by a user on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FiatAccount entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this fiat account. (Relationship: User 1:N FiatAccount)

accountType.description: The type of the fiat account (e.g., checking, savings).

accountNumber.description: The unique account number for the fiat account.

balance.description: The current balance of the fiat account.

currency.description: The currency of the fiat account (e.g., USD).

status.description: The current operational status of the fiat account (e.g., active, suspended).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the fiat account was created.

FiatTransaction.title: FiatTransaction
FiatTransaction.description: Records a transaction made through a fiat account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FiatTransaction entity.

accountId.description: Reference to the FiatAccount involved in this transaction. (Relationship: FiatAccount 1:N FiatTransaction)

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated or is affected by this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N FiatTransaction)

type.description: The type of fiat transaction (e.g., deposit, withdrawal, transfer).

amount.description: The amount of money involved in the transaction.

description.description: A brief description of the transaction.

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., pending, completed, failed).

counterpartyName.description: The name of the other party involved in the transaction.

counterpartyAccount.description: The account number of the other party involved in the transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction was created.

CryptoWallet.title: CryptoWallet
CryptoWallet.description: Represents a cryptocurrency wallet managed or tracked by the user on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CryptoWallet entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this crypto wallet. (Relationship: User 1:N CryptoWallet)

walletAddress.description: The public address of the cryptocurrency wallet.

chain.description: The blockchain network this wallet operates on (e.g., ETH, BSC, POLYGON).

label.description: A user-defined label or name for the wallet.

balance.description: The total estimated balance of the wallet in a base currency (e.g., USD).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the crypto wallet record was created.

CryptoHolding.title: CryptoHolding
CryptoHolding.description: Represents a specific cryptocurrency token held within a user's crypto wallet.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CryptoHolding entity.

walletId.description: Reference to the CryptoWallet holding this token. (Relationship: CryptoWallet 1:N CryptoHolding)

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this holding. (Relationship: User 1:N CryptoHolding)

tokenSymbol.description: The ticker symbol of the cryptocurrency token (e.g., BTC, ETH, USDT).

tokenName.description: The full name of the cryptocurrency token.

balance.description: The quantity of the token held.

avgBuyPrice.description: The average price at which the tokens were acquired.

currentPrice.description: The current market price of the token.

pnl.description: Profit and Loss for this holding, calculated from avgBuyPrice and currentPrice.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when this holding's data (e.g., current price, PnL) was last updated.

CryptoTransaction.title: CryptoTransaction
CryptoTransaction.description: Records a cryptocurrency transaction, such as a buy, sell, transfer, or swap.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CryptoTransaction entity.

walletId.description: Reference to the CryptoWallet involved in this transaction. (Relationship: CryptoWallet 1:N CryptoTransaction)

userId.description: Reference to the User who initiated or is affected by this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N CryptoTransaction)

type.description: The type of cryptocurrency transaction (e.g., buy, sell, transfer, swap).

tokenSymbol.description: The ticker symbol of the cryptocurrency token involved.

amount.description: The quantity of the token transacted.

priceAtExecution.description: The price of the token at the time of transaction execution.

totalValue.description: The total value of the transaction in a base currency (e.g., USD).

txHash.description: The transaction hash, a unique identifier on the blockchain.

status.description: The current status of the blockchain transaction (e.g., pending, confirmed, failed).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the crypto transaction was created.

TradingBot.title: TradingBot
TradingBot.description: Configuration and status for an automated cryptocurrency trading bot created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TradingBot entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this trading bot. (Relationship: User 1:N TradingBot)

name.description: A user-defined name for the trading bot.

strategy.description: The trading strategy employed by the bot (e.g., DCA, grid, arbitrage, momentum).

status.description: The current operational status of the trading bot (e.g., active, paused, stopped).

tradingPair.description: The cryptocurrency trading pair the bot operates on (e.g., BTC/USD).

investmentAmount.description: The total amount invested in this bot's strategy.

frequency.description: The frequency at which the bot executes trades (e.g., daily, hourly, continuous).

takeProfitPct.description: The percentage profit target at which the bot should sell.

stopLossPct.description: The percentage loss threshold at which the bot should sell to limit losses.

totalProfit.description: The total profit generated by the bot since its inception.

totalTrades.description: The total number of trades executed by the bot.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trading bot was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the trading bot's configuration or status was last updated.

BotTrade.title: BotTrade
BotTrade.description: Records an individual trade executed by an automated trading bot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BotTrade entity.

botId.description: Reference to the TradingBot that executed this trade. (Relationship: TradingBot 1:N BotTrade)

type.description: The type of trade executed (e.g., buy, sell).

amount.description: The quantity of the asset traded.

price.description: The execution price of the trade.

profit.description: The profit or loss generated by this specific trade.

status.description: The status of the trade (e.g., executed, pending, failed).

executedAt.description: Timestamp when the trade was executed.

InvestmentPackage.title: InvestmentPackage
InvestmentPackage.description: Defines a pre-configured investment offering available to users on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvestmentPackage entity.

name.description: The name of the investment package (e.g., 'Stable Yield Fund').

description.description: A detailed description of the investment package.

type.description: The type of investment package (e.g., staking, yield farming, fixed deposit).

apyRate.description: The Annual Percentage Yield (APY) offered by the package.

minAmount.description: The minimum investment amount required for this package.

maxAmount.description: The maximum investment amount allowed for this package.

lockPeriodDays.description: The number of days the investment is locked.

riskLevel.description: The assessed risk level of the investment package (e.g., low, medium, high).

status.description: The current status of the package (e.g., active, discontinued).

UserInvestment.title: UserInvestment
UserInvestment.description: Records a user's enrollment and participation in an InvestmentPackage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserInvestment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made this investment. (Relationship: User 1:N UserInvestment)

packageId.description: Reference to the InvestmentPackage the user enrolled in. (Relationship: InvestmentPackage 1:N UserInvestment)

amountInvested.description: The amount of money or crypto invested by the user.

startDate.description: The date when the investment started.

endDate.description: The expected or actual end date of the investment period.

expectedReturn.description: The projected return on investment based on the package's terms.

actualReturn.description: The actual return received from the investment upon completion or withdrawal.

status.description: The current status of the user's investment (e.g., active, completed, withdrawn).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user investment record was created.

WalletConnection.title: WalletConnection
WalletConnection.description: Records a user's connection to an external Web3 wallet (e.g., MetaMask).

id.description: Unique identifier for the WalletConnection entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this wallet connection. (Relationship: User 1:N WalletConnection)

walletType.description: The type of Web3 wallet connected (e.g., metamask, walletconnect, coinbase).

walletAddress.description: The public address of the connected Web3 wallet.

chainId.description: The blockchain chain ID to which the wallet is connected.

connectedAt.description: Timestamp when the wallet was first connected.

lastUsedAt.description: Timestamp when the wallet connection was last actively used.

isActive.description: Indicates if this wallet connection is currently active or enabled by the user.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles and KYC data. Access to a user's document is restricted to the authenticated user themselves. Denormalized 'id' field matches the '{userId}' wildcard for direct authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Manages traditional banking (fiat) accounts for each user. Access is limited to the owner of the user ID in the path. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for robust authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this fiat account.

params.name: fiatAccountId
params.description: The unique ID of the fiat account.

definition.description: Records individual transactions associated with a user's specific fiat account. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for direct authorization without 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the associated fiat account.

params.name: fiatAccountId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent fiat account.

params.name: fiatTransactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the fiat transaction.

definition.description: Stores cryptocurrency wallets managed by a user. Access is limited to the owner of the user ID in the path. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for robust authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this crypto wallet.

params.name: cryptoWalletId
params.description: The unique ID of the crypto wallet.

definition.description: Details specific cryptocurrency token holdings within a user's crypto wallet. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for direct authorization without 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the associated crypto wallet.

params.name: cryptoWalletId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent crypto wallet.

params.name: cryptoHoldingId
params.description: The unique ID of the crypto holding.

definition.description: Records cryptocurrency transactions for a user's specific crypto wallet. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for direct authorization without 'get()' calls.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the associated crypto wallet.

params.name: cryptoWalletId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent crypto wallet.

params.name: cryptoTransactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the crypto transaction.

definition.description: Manages automated trading bots configured by a user. Access is limited to the owner of the user ID in the path. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for robust authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this trading bot.

params.name: tradingBotId
params.description: The unique ID of the trading bot.

definition.description: Records individual trades executed by a user's specific trading bot. CRITICAL: Documents in this collection MUST include a denormalized 'userId' field (matching the path's userId) for authorization independence, as it is not present in the provided schema.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent trading bot.

params.name: tradingBotId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent trading bot.

params.name: botTradeId
params.description: The unique ID of the bot trade.

definition.description: Contains publicly available, predefined investment packages. This collection is globally readable by all authenticated users and represents static, application-wide data.

params.name: packageId
params.description: The unique ID of the investment package.

definition.description: Tracks a user's enrollment and participation in specific investment packages. Access is limited to the owner of the user ID in the path. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for robust authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who made this investment.

params.name: userInvestmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the user's investment record.

definition.description: Records a user's connections to external Web3 wallets (e.g., MetaMask). Access is limited to the owner of the user ID in the path. Documents include denormalized 'userId' for robust authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this wallet connection.

params.name: walletConnectionId
params.description: The unique ID of the wallet connection.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. All user-specific data is organized hierarchically under a `/users/{userId}` root path. This strategy ensures that ownership is explicitly encoded in the document path, making security rules simple and highly performant.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
For all user-owned collections and their nested subcollections (e.g., `fiatAccounts`, `cryptoWallets`, `tradingBots`, `userInvestments`, `walletConnections`, `fiatTransactions`, `cryptoHoldings`, `cryptoTransactions`, `botTrades`), the `userId` is either explicitly present in the document's path or, for deeply nested entities, denormalized as a field within the document itself. This is crucial for collections like `BotTrade`, where the `userId` is not intrinsically part of the schema but is vital for authorization. By having `resource.data.userId` readily available in every relevant document, security rules can evaluate access requests without relying on `get()` calls to parent documents. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations (transactions/batches) and significantly simplifying rule debugging.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) through Structural Segregation:**
Security rules are designed to enforce authorization at the data storage level, not to filter results after an overly broad query. This is achieved through **Structural Segregation**. User-specific data is strictly confined to `/users/{userId}/...` paths. When a user queries `fiatAccounts` for their own ID (e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/fiatAccounts`), the query inherently targets only their owned documents. The `request.auth.uid` is matched against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, ensuring that `list` operations naturally return only authorized results without needing additional rule-based filtering. For global entities like `InvestmentPackage`, which are intended to be publicly readable, they reside in a top-level collection (`/investmentPackages`). This clear separation of homogeneous security postures for different data types simplifies rules, making `list` operations efficient and secure by design.
